meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve 'Stuck' App Syndrome
content.seo_description: Break out of the tutorial loop. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a professional portfolio that lands you high-impact software roles.
content.answer_block: To escape the tutorial loop, shift from passive watching to intentional architecture. Use Antigravity to scaffold complex, real-world systems and Claude Code to iterate on production-grade logic. By focusing on solving specific, user-centric problems rather than following generic tutorials, you build a portfolio that demonstrates actual engineering depth and commercial viability.
content.intro:
You’ve spent months chasing the 'perfect' tutorial, yet your GitHub is a graveyard of To-Do lists and Weather apps. The screen flickers with boilerplate code that feels hollow—you aren't building; you're just transcribing. This stagnation isn't a lack of talent, but a lack of architecture. You are stuck in the shallow end of the pool because you haven't yet learned to design for impact.

Today, we pivot. We are moving from 'student code' to 'production-ready engineering.' By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will transform your workflow. We aren't just building another app; we are building a career-defining portfolio piece that proves you can navigate complexity and ship real-world value.

steps.title: Define the Problem Scope
steps.description: Identify a niche pain point in your daily workflow to ensure your project has genuine utility beyond a classroom assignment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Help me define a scope for a SaaS tool that solves [insert problem]. Provide a PRD, a database schema in Prisma, and a component architecture that avoids over-engineering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review this PRD and generate the project directory structure for a production-ready Next.js app.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code, ensuring you follow modern industry standards like type-safety and modular design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate for a full-stack application using Next.js 14, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include authentication, a clean folder structure, and a mock API endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project boilerplate generated by Antigravity and install all necessary dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Focus on the most difficult feature first. Use Claude Code to write robust functions that handle edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, testable service function for [feature] that handles errors gracefully and adheres to clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the core service logic in /services/feature.ts, adding unit tests for edge cases.'

steps.title: Refine the User Experience
steps.description: Transform functional code into a polished interface by using Claude Code to apply design system constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest UI/UX improvements for a dashboard view, focusing on accessibility and mobile-first responsiveness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the dashboard UI to use a grid layout, ensuring all buttons are accessible and the design is responsive.'

steps.title: Document and Deploy
steps.description: Professional engineering requires documentation. Use tools to generate a README that explains the 'why' behind your technical decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional technical README for this project. Include a system diagram, installation instructions, and a section on the technical challenges solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a comprehensive README.md file based on the implemented project architecture and features.'

pro_tips: Always prioritize 'Data-First' design: define your database schema before writing a single line of frontend code.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your own PRs to catch security vulnerabilities before they reach production.
In your portfolio, document the 'Hard Problems'—the bugs you faced and the specific tradeoffs you made—not just the features you built.
Treat your commits like a professional log; use conventional commits to show you understand team-based version control standards.
Build 'Vertical Slices': complete one end-to-end feature (from DB to UI) at a time rather than building the whole UI first.
content.next_steps: Once your project is deployed, contribute to one open-source library that uses the same stack to deepen your understanding of large-scale codebases.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for tutorials and start building for your future. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap to ship something that matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
